summary: MongoDB Persistence, Request History, and Notification Features - All tests PASSED. Tested MongoDB storage for kiosk requests, request history dialog in kiosk, employee filter, approve/reject notification triggers, and status badges.
seed_data_creation: 7 initial requests seeded in MongoDB on first load (kiosk_requests collection)
context_for_next_testing_agent: MongoDB persistence fully working. Data stored in house_of_veritas.kiosk_requests collection. Request history dialog shows employee's own requests with status badges. Notifications are MOCKED (Twilio not configured) but function calls are logged to console.

mongodb_persistence.details: GET/POST/PATCH all return storage: mongodb. Data persists in MongoDB collection.

request_history_dialog.details: My Requests button visible in kiosk. Dialog shows employee's own requests with status badges.

employee_filter.details: GET /api/kiosk/requests?employeeId=X returns only that employee's requests

status_badges.details: Pending (yellow), Approved (green), Rejected (red) badges displayed correctly

rejection_notes.details: Rejected requests show rejection notes in red background

approval_notes.details: Approved requests show approval notes in green background

notification_on_approve.details: sendNotification called on approve - SMS simulated (Twilio not configured)

notification_on_reject.details: sendNotification called on reject - SMS simulated (Twilio not configured)

data_persistence_verification.details: Created requests persist and are retrievable via GET. MongoDB ObjectIds used.

api_endpoints_tested.POST /api/kiosk/requests: PASS - Creates request in MongoDB, returns storage: mongodb

mocked_apis.notification_sms: MOCKED - Twilio not configured, SMS simulated with console logs
